commit.message:
Task 14: Token auth client-side guardrails (#16)

* Task 14: feat: add token auth client-side guardrails

Add checkTokenWarnings(config) that warns via console.warn (bypassing
logger) for short tokens (<16 chars) and tokens used on HTTPS public
origins. Integrated into main.js init flow before first fetch.

Bump gzip budget from 4608 to 5120 for new module.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback for token warnings

- Wrap checkTokenWarnings body in try/catch (never-throws contract)
- Use strict null check (== null) so empty-string tokens get warned
- Add IPv6 loopback [::1] and RFC 1918 ranges to _isSafeHostname
- Update docstring to document coverage scope
- DRY tests: extract VALID_TOKEN, SHORT_TOKEN_MSG, PUBLIC_ORIGIN_MSG
- Collapse safe-hostname tests into it.each with additional cases
- Add tests: empty-string token, undefined token, never-throws guard

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix: remove config.path from chat.mjs to restore /.netlify/functions/chat route

Netlify Functions v2 with config.path removes the legacy invocation path
(/.netlify/functions/<name>), causing the redirect in netlify.toml to 404.
Removing the config export lets the function be available at the legacy path
that the redirect points to.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- ContextMenu: early return guard for Escape key handler
- PrRepoGroup/SessionList: indexOf→includes, cache toLowerCase outside loops
- SearchableSelect: remove dead toggle(), type bind:this refs as $state
- SessionList: remove duplicate terminalSessionMenu(), reuse activeSessionMenu()
- api.ts: remove unnecessary Content-Type header on bodyless POST
- sessions.ts: remove unused AGENT_YOLO_ARGS constant

commit.message:
Store full email body and attachments; outstanding balance in capital call PDF; email log UI

- New migration 048: email_log gains body (TEXT) and status columns; new
  email_attachments table (BYTEA) for storing sent attachments
- send-email! now always persists body+attachments before attempting SMTP,
  then marks status as sent/failed; existing rows backfilled as 'sent'
- Capital call PDF includes "Plus Previous Capital Call Outstanding Balance" line
  (sum of prior calls minus receipts per investor)
- Capital call commitment filter: exclude commitments with future start dates
- PDF currency column: "$" for USD, currency label otherwise
- Email log frontend: status column with colored badges; envelope icon to view
  email detail in modal (headers + sandboxed HTML body + attachment downloads)
- Tracking pixel stripped from email body in both backend response and frontend
  rendering to prevent false open tracking
- Attachment download via XHR with credentials for cross-origin dev support

commit.message:
Herschrijf data-fetching: verwijder authLoading guard en useFetchOnMount

De authLoading guard blokkeerde data-fetching terwijl de Supabase client
tokens onafhankelijk beheert. Nu gebruikt elk component een simpele useEffect
met cancelled-flag, error checking op elke Supabase query, en alleen
stabiele primitieven als dependencies (orgId string ipv object-referenties).

Wijzigingen in 10 bestanden:
- Verwijder alle useFetchOnMount imports en gebruik
- Verwijder authLoading guard uit alle data-fetching effecten
- Voeg error checking toe aan elke Supabase query
- Gebruik useCallback voor refetch-functies bij CRUD-componenten
- Voeg cancelled-flag cleanup toe aan alle useEffect hooks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix clock inaccuracy: always sync times from server, use clockRef for optimistic updates

  server tick so Timer always has an accurate base for interpolation
- Use clockRef.current times (updated every second) instead of stale
  gameState times in tryLocalMove optimistic calculation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Track E: BackwardBitReader.readBits totalBitsRemaining tracking — prove exact bit consumption (#803)

* feat: BackwardBitReader.readBits totalBitsRemaining tracking

Prove that readBits decreases totalBitsRemaining by exactly n bits:
- readBits_go_totalBitsRemaining: recursive helper decreases by k
- readBits_go_totalBitsRemaining_ge: success implies enough bits
- readBits_totalBitsRemaining_sub: public theorem, exact consumption
- readBits_totalBitsRemaining_lt: strict decrease for n > 0

These complete the readBits specification trifecta alongside
readBits_value_lt_pow2 (value bound) and the existing edge-case
theorems, enabling downstream proofs for Huffman decoding progress,
FSE state machine correctness, and sequence decoding bit budgets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: progress entry for readBits totalBitsRemaining tracking

  (e.g. minMcap 2_000 → 2_000_000_000 for us_large, etc.)
- Added per-universe cap (top N by dollarVolume) so screener stays practical:
  us_large=500, us_mid=400, eu=400, etf=200, apac=300, all=600
- Added crypto universe (hardcoded 25 Yahoo Finance symbols: BTC-USD, ETH-USD…)
  StockAnalysis doesn't list crypto, so it remains a static list
- Added saToYahoo() converter: StockAnalysis uses EXCHANGE/TICKER format for
  international stocks (e.g. ETR/SAP, EPA/MC, STO/INVE.B). Convert to Yahoo
  Finance format (SAP.DE, MC.PA, INVE-B.ST) using 25-exchange mapping table.
  Share-class dots converted to dashes (INVE.B → INVE-B.ST).
  Unknown exchanges return null and are filtered out.
- getWithMeta() now includes yahooSymbol field alongside original SA symbol

screener.js: remove unused 'today' variable

index.js: remove unused 'force' param from export_parquet, update universe description

test-e2e.js: 40 tests (was 37) — added:
- get("eu") returns Yahoo-format symbols (.DE, .PA, etc.)
- get("crypto") returns 25 hardcoded symbols
- saToYahoo() converts ETR/SAP→SAP.DE, EPA/MC→MC.PA, STO/INVE.B→INVE-B.ST,
  AAPL→AAPL (US unchanged), OSL/EQNR→EQNR.OL, XXX/FOO→null (unknown)
- getWithMeta() has yahooSymbol field

feat(gate1): billing, withdrawal, heartbeat rate limit

- Pre-pay billing: renter balance checked at job submit, deducted upfront
- Settlement on job completion: refund unused or charge extra based on actual duration
- Renter top-up endpoint (POST /api/renters/topup) with 1000 SAR max
- Renter balance check (GET /api/renters/balance) with held amount tracking
- Provider earnings check (GET /api/providers/earnings) with withdrawal tracking
- Provider withdrawal request (POST /api/providers/withdraw) with 10 SAR minimum
- Withdrawals table in SQLite (withdrawal_id, amount_sar, payout_method, status)
- Heartbeat rate limit: 4/min per IP (daemon sends every 30s = 2/min normally)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(zed): auto-launch PrisM sidecar when gateway is unreachable

If Zed's PrisM provider gets a connection-refused error on the /models
fetch, it now finds the prism binary (via $PRISM_BIN, PATH, or
~/.cargo/bin/prism), spawns it with --port <port>, and retries the
fetch up to 5× with 1500ms backoff. The sidecar is killed automatically
when the State is dropped (Zed exit). The config view shows a status
line: "running (sidecar)", "running (external)", or "binary not found".

fix: disable auto-notarization and manually sign all binaries

- Add APPLE_NOTARIZE: false to disable Tauri's auto-notarization
- Deep sign all .node, .dylib, and executable files recursively
- Sign specific problematic binaries (node, ripgrep) in nested directories
- Re-sign entire app bundle with Developer ID certificate
- Rebuild DMG after signing to include signed binaries
- Manually notarize the DMG using xcrun notarytool

This fixes the notarization failure caused by unsigned binaries in the app bundle.

fix: usar primaryKey(autoIncrement: false) em colunas UUID

Add `suggested_next_action` from judgment agent to execution context for next iteration. This allows the execution agent to receive actionable feedback when a task remains incomplete, enabling it to pursue fundamentally different approaches rather than repeating the same strategy.

Changes:

- Inject judgment feedback into execution agent prompt
- Store and forward `suggested_next_action` across iterations
- Add comprehensive test coverage for feedback mechanism

Support multiple time slots per event

Replace single time field with times array — admin dialog allows adding/removing time slots dynamically. Displayed as '19:00 & 21:00' on site.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: replace serverless-http with native Netlify streaming for real-time thinking

serverless-http buffers the entire Express response before sending it to the
client, causing all SSE events (thinking tokens, content tokens) to arrive at
once — making thinking appear as "Thought for 0.0s" with no progressive display.

- Add netlify/functions/chat.mjs: a Netlify Functions v2 handler that returns a
  native ReadableStream Response, streaming SSE tokens to the browser in real-time
- Route /api/chat to the new function in netlify.toml (before the generic /api/*)
- Add live thinking timer in chat.js that ticks via requestAnimationFrame while
  reasoning tokens stream, then freezes to final elapsed time when content begins

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: implement repository selection with GitHub integration

Add full repository management flow: users can browse their GitHub repos,
track up to 8, and manage selections with optimistic local UI updates and
a refresh button to re-fetch from GitHub.

Backend: Octokit-based getUserRepos with pagination, Drizzle ORM queries
for CRUD with cascade deletes, Zod validation pipe, JwtAuthGuard on all
endpoints. Frontend: Pinia store with local cache (add/remove update state
without re-fetching), RepositoriesView with grouped-by-owner layout and
search filter, dashboard CTA for empty state.

Also fixes: login page redirect loop (401 handler), auth controller test
assertion, tsconfig paths for @gitdbt/shared runtime resolution.
